name: PixiJS (2D WebGL Rendering Engine)

performance.notes: WebGL GPU-accelerated 2D; 60fps with thousands of sprites

agent_readiness.notes: Fast 2D WebGL rendering engine. Application, Container, Sprite, Graphics, Text, AnimatedSprite, TilingSprite. Asset loader with WEBP/PNG/SVG. Filters and shaders. Particle systems. Interaction events. v8 uses WebGPU + WebGL2. Used for games, interactive data viz, ads, UI effects. React-pixi for declarative API. Spine animation integration. 4x faster than Canvas 2D API for complex scenes. Phaser.js uses PixiJS renderer. Alternative to Konva.js for canvas.

gotchas: v8 has breaking changes from v7
WebGPU requires modern browser for best performance
best_when: Games and interactive experiences needing 2D GPU rendering
Interactive UI effects (particles, shaders) beyond CSS animation
Canvas-based apps with thousands of interactive elements
summary: PixiJS is the fastest 2D WebGL/WebGPU rendering engine for browsers. Sprite, Container, Graphics, Text, particle systems with GPU acceleration. v8 adds WebGPU support. react-pixi for declarative React API. Used for games, interactive data viz, and UI effects needing 60fps with thousands of elements. Spine animation support. Use Three.js/Babylon.js for 3D, CSS/Framer Motion for simple UI animations.
